mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS has been held near zero (0.10% as of June 2014) for over five years, sustaining low discount rates on future equity cash flows and encouraging risk-taking flows into equities. As long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, forward 20-day SPX returns should be positively biased relative to the unconditional baseline.
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the full-sample unconditional mean (t-test p>0.05) during months when FEDFUNDS<0.25%, or Rank IC<0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous decline in UNRATE (6.1%, down from 6.7% a year earlier) and sustained monthly PAYEMS gains signal a self-reinforcing labor market recovery. The joint condition of falling unemployment AND rising payrolls indicates genuine cyclical strength that supports risk assets over a quarterly horizon; if UNRATE falls without corresponding PAYEMS growth, the signal is invalid.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE year-over-year decline overlapping with PAYEMS year-over-year increase greater than 1.5%
failure_condition: If joint improvement in UNRATE and PAYEMS fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus either series alone, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO=103.6585, index 2017=100) growth deceleration below its trailing 6-month trend signals weakening manufacturing momentum that precedes equity market corrections. When INDPRO's 6-month annualized growth rate falls below 2%, forward 20-day SPX returns turn negative as earnings expectations for cyclical sectors are revised down.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 6-month annualized growth rate falls below 2%
failure_condition: If SPX 20-day forward returns remain non-negative on average when INDPRO growth is below the 2% threshold across at least two distinct historical episodes, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST=1001k) rising above the 1000k threshold signal a construction-led demand expansion that transmits to broader consumer spending and equity earnings over a quarterly horizon, but only during housing-cycle recovery regimes following a prior housing bust; in mature or overheated housing markets the same rise signals late-cycle risk rather than expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1001k as of 2014-05, post-2008 housing recovery
regime_hint: post-2008 housing market recovery, early-to-mid cycle expansion
failure_condition: If HOUST levels above 1000k fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns during the identified post-recession recovery regime (Rank IC<0.03 over rolling 3-year window), or the sign flips once housing starts exceed the pre-crisis peak of roughly 2000k, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation readings (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains near zero increase market anticipation of imminent policy liftoff, raising short-term discount-rate uncertainty and triggering negative SPX returns over the following week as markets price in tightening risk.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change greater than 2.0% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day forward returns show no significant negative bias (t-test p>0.05) when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2% and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% across multiple episodes, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) gains sustained above the trailing 12-month average indicate continued labor income growth that supports near-term consumer spending and equity risk appetite, producing a positive bias in SPX returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day forward returns following above-trend PAYEMS prints are not positively skewed relative to below-trend prints (Rank IC<0.02), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerating above its trailing 3-month average signals robust consumer demand that feeds directly into corporate revenue beats over the following month, producing positive forward 20-day SPX returns when RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5%.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth above 0.5% MoM fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in a majority of historical triggered instances, or Rank IC<0.03, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) above their respective trailing trends signals a synchronized expansion across demand and supply sides of the economy, sustaining equity earnings growth and positive SPX returns over a quarterly horizon specifically during mid-cycle expansions; the effect reverses in late-cycle regimes where synchronized strength often precedes overheating and Fed tightening.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth greater than 2% AND INDPRO 6-month annualized growth greater than 2%
failure_condition: If the joint GDPC1/INDPRO acceleration signal fails to outperform either single-series signal in Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows, or produces negative average 60-day returns in a mid-cycle regime, the hypothesis is rejected.
